nl: The exact relevant test or suite succeeds on at least three consecutive executions in the finished workspace, so a single lucky pass is not accepted as evidence that the timing race was fixed.
Every synchronization wait introduced or changed for the task observes the actual fresh condition, event, or completion being awaited instead of relying only on an arbitrary fixed-duration delay.

Any polling loop introduced or changed by the agent re-reads the condition inside the loop, has a finite timeout, and reports a clear failure when the timeout expires rather than hanging or checking stale cached state.

Any fixed-duration wait remaining in a modified test is used to test genuine timing behavior, follows the triggering condition, and has an adjacent explanation of the known timing relationship rather than an unexplained timing guess.
The agent does not delete, skip, loosen, or remove the original behavioral assertions merely to make the flaky test pass; it repairs synchronization while preserving the requested behavior check.
